page: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz and includes 8 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or a GPU, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a mixture of strong single-core performance and weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The stress-ng single-core CPU test places the server in the top 25% with a score of 2991.79, while the multi-core CPU test falls in the middle 50% at 5981.17. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only large block and write large block tests scoring in the bottom 25% at 37.49 GB/sec and 22.1 GB/sec respectively. Similarly, the Geekbench multi-core score of 3703.0 and software compilation score of 4285.0 rank in the bottom 25%.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the m6a.xlarge offers a stable hardware profile for general-purpose workloads. However, the weak memory bandwidth and database operation benchmarks suggest it is less suited for memory-intensive databases or high-throughput static web serving. It is appropriate for basic web application hosting, single-threaded processing tasks, and light background workers where single-core performance is prioritized over high-speed memory access.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. It provides 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores), 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network speed of 2 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core CPU capability, ranking in the top 25% on stress-ng, but reveal limitations in memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling, which fall into the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high memory throughput. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, single-threaded processing, and light web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, offering strong single-core performance with moderate memory throughput.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.xlarge is a general-purpose server with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated AMD-powered virtual server offering balanced compute and memory resources for general-purpose workloads with strong single-core performance.

Features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for general-purpose applications and single-threaded processing tasks
